free_text: final version of data from priv.
communication.
Gamma-rays from inelastic scattering of neutrons by
Fe, Cu, Zn, Ge and Se
2 MV Van de Graaff accelerator.
D(d,n)He-3, gas target, 1 cm in diam. and
3 cm long, 40 cm-Hg, Ni-foil (1.27*1E-3 millim. thick)
as entrance window.
The spread in the energy of the neutrons
.Ring shape, 9 cm inner and 11 cm outer
diameter, 5 cm thick.
NaI(Tl) crystal, 2 inches in diam. x 2 inch.
BF3 long counter used for monitoring neutron flux
.The neutron flux was calculated from the published
data on the differential cross-sections of d(d,n)He-3
reaction ( see REL-REF,J,RMP,28,103).
Later these cross-sections were revised by the same
authors (see REL-REF,B,FAST N.PH.,1,73).
Accordingly, the cross-sections given in the reference
have been renormalized by multiplying them   with a
factor = 1/1.1,these renormalized values are given here
Gamma-rays from (n,n'gamma) reactions.
.De-excitation scheme and isotopic assignment given in
reference.
.Absorption of gamma-rays in the sample.
Total uncertainty includes:
The cross-section error originates mainly
in the determination of the gamma-ray yield and the
neutron flux.
Uncertainty in determination of area under
a weak or unresolved photo-peak.
The standard error in the effective photo-peak
efficiency
The statistical accuracy of gamma-ray
counting was 1% or better.
* The errors due to background subtraction were
negligible for large or isolated photo-peaks.
Final data from Dr K.Nishimura, 10/1971.
Approved by the author.
A.P.T. Japan code changed to JPN
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
BIB in Subent 001 update. DATA-ERR -> ERR-T. COS->ANG.
REACTION code corrected in Subents 002-006. Meaningless
zeros were deleted from the values in Subents 002-006.
EN-RSL moved to COMMON section in Subent 001.
REL-REF, INC-SPECT were added. EN-RSL -> EN-RSL-HW.
SD: SF4=Fe-0 was added to REACTION code.
Meaningless zeros were deleted from the values.
DATA-ERR -> ERR-T. EN-RSL moved to Subent 001.

Meaningless zeros were deleted from the values.

Meaningless zeros were deleted from the values.
